title: The Demon-Ship.
content:
В В В В 'Twas off the Wash - the sun went down - the sea look'd black and grim,
В В В В For stormy clouds, with murky fleece, were mustering at the brim;
В В В В Titanic shades! enormous gloom! - as if the solid night
В В В В Of Erebus rose suddenly to seize upon the light!
В В В В It was a time for mariners to bear a wary eye
В В В В With such a dark conspiracy between the sea and sky!

В В В В Down went my-helm - close reef'd - the tack held freely in my hand - 
В В В В With ballast snug - I put about, and scudded for the land.
В В В В Loud hiss'd the sea beneath her lee - my little boat flew fast,
В В В В But faster still the rushing storm came borne upon the blast.
В В В В Lord! what a roaring hurricane beset the straining sail!
В В В В What furious sleet, with level drift, and fierce assaults of hail!

В В В В What darksome caverns yawn'd before! what jagged steeps behind!
В В В В Like battle-steeds, with foamy manes, wild tossing in the wind.
В В В В Each after each sank down astern, exhausted in the chase,
В В В В But where it sank another rose and galloped in its place;
В В В В As black as night - they turned to white, and cast against the cloud
В В В В A snowy sheet, as if each surge upturned a sailor's shroud: - 
В В В В Still flew my boat; alas! alas! her course was nearly run!
В В В В Behold yon fatal billow rise - ten billows heap'd in one!

В В В В With fearful speed the dreary mass came rolling, rolling, fast,
В В В В As if the scooping sea contain'd one only wave at last!
В В В В Still on it came, with horrid roar, a swift pursuing grave;
В В В В It seem'd as though some cloud had turned its hugeness to a wave!
В В В В Its briny sleet began to beat beforehand in my face - 
В В В В I felt the rearward keel begin to climb its swelling base!
В В В В I saw its alpine hoary head impending over mine!
В В В В Another pulse - and down it rush'd - an avalanche of brine!
В В В В Brief pause had I, on God to cry, or think of wife and home;
В В В В The waters clos'd - and when I shriek'd, I shriek'd below the foam!
В В В В Beyond that rush I have no hint of any after deed - 
В В В В For I was tossing on the waste, as senseless as a weed.

В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *

В В В В "Where am I? in the breathing world, or in the world of death?"
В В В В With sharp and sudden pang I drew another birth of breath;
В В В В My eyes drank in a doubtful light, my ears a doubtful sound - 
В В В В And was that ship a real ship whose tackle seem'd around?
В В В В A moon, as if the earthly moon, was shining up aloft;
В В В В But were those beams the very beams that I had seen so oft?
В В В В A face, that mock'd the human face, before me watch'd alone;
В В В В But were those eyes the eyes of man that look'd against my own?

В В В В Oh! never may the moon again disclose me such a sight
В В В В As met my gaze, when first I look'd, on that accursed night!
В В В В I've seen a thousand horrid shapes begot of fierce extremes
В В В В Of fever; and most frightful things have haunted in my dreams - 
В В В В Hyenas - cats - blood-loving bats - and apes with hateful stare, - 
В В В В Pernicious snakes, and shaggy bulls - the lion, and she-bear - 
В В В В Strong enemies, with Judas looks, of treachery and spite - 
В В В В Detested features, hardly dimm'd and banish'd by the light!
В В В В Pale-sheeted ghosts, with gory locks, upstarting from their tombs - 
В В В В All phantasies and images that flit in midnight glooms - 
В В В В Hags, goblins, demons, lemures, have made me all aghast, - 
В В В В But nothing like that GRIMLY ONE who stood beside the mast!

В В В В His cheek was black - his brow was black - his eyes and hair as dark;
В В В В His hand was black, and where it touch'd, it left a sable mark;
В В В В His throat was black, his vest the same, and when I look'd beneath,
В В В В His breast was black - all, all, was black, except his grinning teeth.
В В В В His sooty crew were like in hue, as black as Afric slaves!
В В В В Oh, horror! e'en the ship was black that plough'd the inky waves!

В В В В "Alas!" I cried, "for love of truth and blessed mercy's sake,
В В В В Where am I? in what dreadful ship? upon what dreadful lake?"
В В В В "What shape is that, so very grim, and black as any coal?
В В В В It is Mahound, the Evil One, and he has gain'd my soul!
В В В В Oh, mother dear! my tender nurse! dear meadows that beguil'd
В В В В My happy days, when I was yet a little sinless child, - 
В В В В My mother dear - my native fields, I never more shall see:
В В В В I'm sailing in the Devil's Ship, upon the Devil's Sea!"

В В В В Loud laugh'd that SABLE MARINER, and loudly in return
В В В В His sooty crew sent forth a laugh that rang from stem to stern - 
В В В В A dozen pair of grimly cheeks were crumpled on the nonce - 
В В В В As many sets of grinning teeth came shining out at once:
В В В В A dozen gloomy shapes at once enjoy'd the merry fit,
В В В В With shriek and yell, and oaths as well, like Demons of the Pit.
В В В В They crow'd their fill, and then the Chief made answer for the whole; - 
В В В В "Our skins," said he, "are black, ye see, because we carry coal;
В В В В You'll find your mother sure enough, and see your native fields - 
В В В В For this here ship has pick'd you up - the Mary Ann of Shields!"
meaning:
Thomas Hood's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Demon-Ship."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
